name: Cyrus Chestnut
abstract: Cyrus Chestnut (born January 17, 1963) is an American jazz pianist, composer and producer. In 2006, Josh Tyrangiel, music critic for Time, wrote: "What makes Chestnut the best jazz pianist of his generation is a willingness to abandon notes and play space."

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: The rhythm section swings between Cyrus Chestnut or Mulgrew Miller... I was really impressed with his performances when he worked with the Alumni Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I produced three records for him... he and I played together with Terence Blanchard and Donald Harrison.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I played on it, Cyrus Chestnut played piano... and it turned out to be a really marvelous project.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Cyrus Chestnut? [INTERVIEWEE] Cyrus Chestnut, but also the woman piano player, Allen, Geri Allen playing Mary Lou Williams. Each one of them — they didn’t come out and say they were these people but they were modeled on these people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I just heard him recently in Danbury with Faddis and with a wonderful piano player named Cyrus Chestnut.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Genevieve Rose
about_quotes.evidence: Cyrus Chestnut was on piano... I look over at Cyrus... we did the first chorus
about_quotes.doc_title: Genevieve Rose interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998
